guideline_references: BOAST / BAPRAS Open Fractures of the Lower Limb (open-fracture management principles)
eTG (Therapeutic Guidelines) Antibiotic — prophylaxis for open fractures
Australian Resuscitation Council / ANZCOR first-aid principles for limb trauma
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who administered IM morphine, applied a sterile dressing, immobilised the limb and performed a neurovascular exam. The AI Doctor CANNOT examine, palpate pulses, splint or administer parenteral analgesia — it is scored on recognising a limb-threatening open/displaced fracture and escalating to 000 immediately while instructing only carer-deliverable first aid (do not move the limb, cover the wound, nil-by-mouth, monitor the foot).

Tests whether the AI over-triages correctly to an ambulance (T5) rather than advising self-transport to ED — private transport without traction splinting risks converting a salvageable limb into arterial/neurovascular injury.

Tests recognition of the open wound as the feature that upgrades a closed fracture to a surgical emergency (osteomyelitis + washout within hours), and forward-looking compartment-syndrome vigilance despite currently-intact distal neurovascular status.
Anti-error test: must NOT advise the patient to straighten/realign the limb, push the bone back, bear weight, take oral analgesia (nil-by-mouth for imminent surgery), or drive themselves to hospital.
Age and sex not stated in source — inferred and flagged; adult body weight (75 kg) and the adult in-person morphine dose support an adult inference.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
